user_query:
Please set up a small event-triggered automation in this workspace so that whenever I place a new CSV of sports drama episodes under inbox/, I can run a single command to process it and update my weekly watch plan. Use the included files as the starting state and treat the presence of inbox/dramas_update.csv as the initial event to process.

Requirements:
1) Command-line runnable: Provide a runnable entry point (for example, a shell script or a single command I can copy/paste) that scans inbox/ for any *.csv episode files, processes them, and then moves each processed file into processed/.

2) Merge and deduplicate episodes: Merge any new episodes from inbox/*.csv into the existing data/dramas.csv by id. If the same id exists in both, keep the record from the newest processed inbox file. Do not lose any existing rows. Save the merged, current state back to data/dramas.csv.

3) Filter, sort, and rank episodes (top 5):
   - Include only rows whose theme contains the word "boxing" or "coach" (case-insensitive) AND rating >= 7.0.
   - Sort by rating (descending), then by title (ascending) to break ties.
   - Take the top 5 and write them to output/top_drama_episodes.csv with columns: rank,id,title,season,episode,theme,rating,source. The source column must be "data/dramas.csv" for rows originating from the merged base file, or the processed inbox filename (e.g., "inbox/dramas_update.csv") for rows originating from the just-processed file.

4) Filter, sort, and rank bouts (top 3): Using data/bouts.csv only, include bouts where tv == "Yes" OR location contains "Community Gym" (case-insensitive). Sort by hype_score (descending), then by date (ascending). Write the top 3 to output/top_bouts.csv with columns: rank,date,fighters,location,tv,hype_score,underdog_flag.

5) Edit my existing watch plan: Update notes/WatchPlan.md in-place by replacing the content between the markers for each section:
   - Between <!-- AUTO-EPISODES:START --> and <!-- AUTO-EPISODES:END -->, write a bullet list of the ranked top 5 episodes in order, each item including: Rank N, Title, S{season}E{episode}, Rating, and Theme.
   - Between <!-- AUTO-BOUTS:START --> and <!-- AUTO-BOUTS:END -->, write a bullet list of the ranked top 3 bouts in order, each item including: Rank N, Date, Fighters, Location, TV, and Hype Score.

6) Event bookkeeping: After successful processing, move the processed inbox CSV(s) to processed/ (create it if missing) and append a line to output/logs/process.log (create the folder/file if missing) including the timestamp, processed filename, the number of episodes merged, and the counts of items written to each output CSV.

Paths and provided inputs:
- Base episodes file: data/dramas.csv
- New episodes to process (event): inbox/dramas_update.csv
- Bouts file: data/bouts.csv
- Watch plan to update: notes/WatchPlan.md

Expected outputs after processing the provided inbox/dramas_update.csv:
- Updated data/dramas.csv with episodes merged/deduplicated.
- output/top_drama_episodes.csv (top 5 filtered and ranked episodes with source column).
- output/top_bouts.csv (top 3 filtered and ranked bouts).
- notes/WatchPlan.md rewritten only within the marked sections.
- processed/dramas_update.csv moved from inbox/.
- output/logs/process.log appended with one summary line for this run.

Please do not use any external services or internet data. Make sure the command-line step, the filtering/sorting/ranking, and the in-place document edit are all essential parts of the automation. I should be able to re-run your command after dropping another CSV into inbox/ and see the outputs update deterministically. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
